The text discusses the role of Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) in modern smart grid systems, focusing on their communication protocols and their potential vulnerabilities. PMUs are devices that measure voltages, currents, and calculate phase angles and the Rate of Change of Frequency (ROCOF) with high accuracy and sampling rate. They are crucial in Wide Area Measurement Systems (WAMS) for quick anomaly detection and preventive measures against large faults.
